subject: health
query_asked: How many Canadians have a family doctor? What's the source?
cited_sources: statcan.gc.ca (Access to health care); cma.ca (OurCare 2025); pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov; cihi.ca
answer_value: StatCan 82.8% have a regular health care provider (2023); OurCare 81% family doctor/NP; 5.9M lack access
note: opposite of Bing on same query: Duck.ai cites StatCan's 82.8% directly and distinguishes it from the OurCare family-doctor-specific 81% — NOT a gap on this engine

answer_value: 56,631 apparent opioid toxicity deaths Jan 2016-Dec 2025; 5,630 in 2025 (PHAC)
statcan_value: external-cause deaths incl. drug poisoning (13100156, 2024) — not opioid-specific
note: consistent with Bing: PHAC owns opioid surveillance; StatCan absent on both engines — genuine cross-org case, not a StatCan underutilization gap
